tools.description: R packages with the data-generating processes and simulated datasets (with known ground-truth effects) from the 2016/2017 Atlantic Causal Inference Conference competitions.
tools.name: ACIC Competition data (aciccomp)

tools.description: Curated collection of reference Bayesian networks (ASIA, ALARM, HEPAR2, etc.) with known ground-truth structure in multiple formats, the standard ground-truth benchmark for structure-learning evaluation.
tools.name: bnlearn Bayesian Network Repository

tools.description: GSK benchmark suite (with curated single-cell perturbation datasets) for evaluating network/causal-graph inference methods from gene-perturbation data.
tools.name: CausalBench

tools.description: R/Python/Stata packages providing the example datasets (LaLonde, NSW, etc.) used in The Effect, Causal Inference: The Mixtape, and What If textbooks.
tools.name: causaldata

tools.description: Reference repo bundling the widely-cited IHDP (Infant Health and Development Program) semi-synthetic benchmark CSVs with known ground-truth treatment effects used across ITE papers.
tools.name: CEVAE datasets (IHDP)

tools.description: Python framework providing standard causal-inference benchmark datasets (IHDP, IBM ACIC) plus synthetic-data generation and baseline comparison for evaluating ITE methods.
tools.name: JustCause

tools.description: Realistic causal-inference benchmark that fits generative models to real data (LaLonde PSID/CPS, Twins) to produce samples with known ground-truth treatment effects.
tools.name: RealCause

tools.description: Standard benchmark of ~100 bivariate cause-effect pairs with ground-truth causal direction for evaluating pairwise causal-discovery methods (Mooij et al. 2016).

tools.description: Python sandbox of dynamic simulators with known ground-truth causal effects for stress-testing causal-inference and sequential decision-making methods.
tools.name: WhyNot

tools.description: Amortized variational inference for causal structure learning (NeurIPS 2022), predicting causal graphs directly from data via a trained neural network.
tools.name: AVICI

tools.description: Snakemake workflow to run, develop and benchmark causal-discovery/structure-learning algorithms across many libraries (bnlearn, pcalg, causal-learn, gCastle, Tetrad, etc.) with data generators and metrics.
tools.name: benchpress

tools.description: Independent Python package (built on pgmpy) for Bayesian network structure learning, parameter learning, inference and sampling.
tools.name: bnlearn (Python)

tools.description: Widely used R package for Bayesian network structure learning (constraint-based, score-based, hybrid), parameter learning and inference.
tools.name: bnlearn (R)

tools.description: Python package for graph and pairwise causal discovery, bridging to R packages (pcalg, bnlearn) and providing deep-learning-based methods.
tools.name: Causal Discovery Toolbox (CDT)

tools.description: Command-line interface wrapping the Tetrad causal-discovery algorithms for running searches on data files from a shell.
tools.name: causal-cmd

tools.description: Comprehensive Python library of classic and state-of-the-art causal discovery algorithms (PC, FCI, GES, LiNGAM, Granger, etc.) for learning causal structure from observational data.
tools.name: causal-learn

tools.description: Python package for creating, manipulating and learning causal DAGs, including GSP/IGSP permutation-based and interventional structure-learning algorithms.
tools.name: causaldag

tools.description: Python package of baseline causal-discovery algorithms and analytics tools (varsortability, sortnregress) for benchmarking structure learning.

tools.description: Python library for learning Bayesian network structure (NOTEARS-based) and reasoning about causal relationships for decision-making.

tools.description: Microsoft's deep-learning library for end-to-end causal discovery and inference, including the DECI amortized causal-discovery model.

tools.description: Python package learning DAGs via continuous optimization using an M-matrix log-determinant acyclicity characterization (DAGMA).

tools.description: PyWhy's experimental causal discovery package providing constraint-based and other global structure-learning algorithms with a scikit-learn-style API.

tools.description: Python causal structure learning toolbox emphasizing gradient-based methods (NOTEARS, GraN-DAG, etc.) plus data simulators and SHD/F1 evaluation metrics.

tools.description: R package (Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation) that recovers group- and individual-level directed contemporaneous/lagged network structure from time series via unified SEM search.

tools.description: Python package implementing the LiNGAM family (ICA-LiNGAM, DirectLiNGAM, VAR-LiNGAM, RCD, etc.) for causal discovery in linear non-Gaussian models.

tools.description: Reference implementation of NO TEARS, casting DAG structure learning as a continuous optimization with a smooth acyclicity constraint.

tools.description: Canonical R package for graphical-model causal structure learning (PC, FCI, RFCI, GIES) and causal effect estimation (IDA).

tools.description: Python toolkit for probabilistic graphical models with Bayesian network structure learning (PC, Hill-Climb, etc.), parameter learning, inference and causal reasoning.

tools.description: Python interface (via JPype) exposing the Java Tetrad causal-discovery algorithms in Python workflows.

tools.description: C++/Python library for probabilistic graphical models (Bayesian networks) with structure learning and causal do-calculus support.

tools.description: Synthetic difference-in-differences estimation with inference and graphics for Stata (Arkhangelsky et al. 2021).
tools.name: sdid

tools.description: Sensitivity analysis to unobserved confounders for OLS via robustness values and contour plots (Cinelli & Hazlett).
tools.name: sensemakr

tools.description: Fits mixed-effect models with spatially correlated random effects (geostatistical and Markov-random-field GLMMs) via Laplace/h-likelihood approximations.
tools.name: spaMM

tools.description: Julia package for spatial weights matrices, spatial-autocorrelation tests (global/local Moran, Geary, Getis-Ord, LISA) and choropleth ESDA.
tools.name: SpatialDependence.jl

tools.description: Utilities for spatial data manipulation, sampling and modelling including autologistic models, spatial smoothing and landscape/point-pattern metrics.
tools.name: spatialEco

tools.description: Estimates spatial cross-sectional lattice/areal models (SAR, SEM, SAC, Durbin) by maximum likelihood, spatial 2SLS and GMM following Cliff-Ord and Kelejian-Prucha.
tools.name: spatialreg

tools.description: Builds spatial weights matrices from contiguities/distances and computes spatial-autocorrelation tests (Moran's I, Geary's C, Getis-Ord, local LISA).
tools.name: spdep

tools.description: Sparse-compatible generalized linear models (Gaussian, Poisson, logistic) serving as the estimation base for PySAL's spint and GWR modules.
tools.name: spglm

tools.description: Fits Cliff-Ord spatial autoregressive models with heteroskedastic innovations via GMM/IV, including spatial HAC standard errors.
tools.name: sphet

tools.description: Calibrates gravity-type spatial interaction models (unconstrained and production/attraction-constrained Poisson) via entropy maximization.
tools.name: spint

tools.description: Maximum-likelihood and GM estimation plus diagnostic testing of fixed/random-effects econometric models for spatial panel data (Millo & Piras).
tools.name: splm

tools.description: Estimates Moran-eigenvector spatial/spatio-temporal regression models with spatially varying coefficients for Gaussian and non-Gaussian data.
tools.name: spmoran

tools.description: Community Stata (SSC) precursor to official Sp: builds spatial-weighting matrices (spmat) and fits SAR/SEM/SAC by ML and GS2SLS (spreg, spivreg) by Drukker, Peng, Prucha & Raciborski.
tools.name: sppack (spreg/spivreg/spmat)

tools.description: PySAL spatial econometric regression: OLS/2SLS with spatial lag and error (SAR/SEM/SARAR/Durbin), GM/ML estimators, panel and regimes models.
tools.name: spreg

tools.description: Tests and estimates spatial Seemingly Unrelated Regression (SUR-SLM/SEM/SDM/SLX) systems by maximum likelihood and three-stage least squares.
tools.name: spsur

tools.description: Finite-population block kriging to predict totals and weighted sums from spatially autocorrelated sample data (Ver Hoef 2008).
tools.name: sptotal

tools.description: Stata module that reweights survey microdata to external aggregate totals using Deville-Sarndal calibration methods.
tools.name: sreweight

tools.description: Provides dplyr-like syntax for computing summary statistics on complex survey data by wrapping the survey package.
tools.name: srvyr

tools.description: Stacked event-study estimator (Cengiz et al.) that builds clean cohort-vs-never-treated stacks to avoid bad TWFE comparisons.
tools.name: stackedev

tools.description: Efficient estimators (Roth & Sant'Anna) for difference-in-differences settings with randomized/as-good-as-random treatment timing.
tools.name: staggered

tools.description: Official Stata (18+) commands estimating Jordà local-projection impulse-response functions, with ivlpirf adding instrumental-variables identification.
tools.name: Stata lpirf / ivlpirf

tools.description: Official Stata Sp suite fitting cross-sectional and panel spatial autoregressive models (SAR/SEM/SAC, with endogenous covariates) by ML and GS2SLS.
tools.name: Stata sp (spregress/spxtregress/spivregress)

tools.description: Official Stata time-series suite estimating reduced-form and structural VARs (var, svar, varbasic) with IRF/FEVD via the irf subsystem.
tools.name: Stata var / svar / varbasic

tools.description: General-purpose statistical modeling library (OLS/GLM, robust/clustered SE, panel and time-series tools); a foundation rather than a quasi-experimental-specific package.
tools.name: statsmodels

tools.description: Analysis of complex survey samples including design-based summary statistics, generalized linear models, calibration and raking of survey weights.
tools.name: survey

tools.description: Data-driven identification of structural VARs (changes in volatility, GARCH, independent-component analysis, non-Gaussian ML) with IRFs and bootstrap inference.
tools.name: svars

tools.description: Stata's built-in survey-data prefix and estimators that account for sampling weights, stratification, and clustering in complex survey designs.
tools.name: svy

tools.description: Quickly and flexibly applies rake weighting to survey data, extending the survey package's weighting interface to correct for non-response.
tools.name: svyweight

tools.description: Classic synthetic control method (Abadie, Diamond & Hainmueller) for comparative case studies with a single treated unit.
tools.name: Synth

tools.description: Original Stata implementation of the synthetic control method (Abadie, Diamond & Hainmueller).
tools.name: synth

tools.description: Automates running synth across treated units/placebos to perform inference and produce synthetic-control plots.
tools.name: synth_runner

tools.description: Pure-Julia synthetic control and synthetic difference-in-differences estimators (beta).
tools.name: SynthControl.jl

tools.description: Reference R implementation of the synthetic difference-in-differences (SDID) estimator of Arkhangelsky et al. (2021).
tools.name: synthdid

tools.description: Python port of synthetic DiD supporting SDID/SC/DiD estimators with bootstrap, placebo and jackknife inference.
tools.name: synthdid.py

tools.description: Python package for classic and Differenced (robust) synthetic control estimation with placebo-based inference.
tools.name: SyntheticControlMethods

tools.description: Nonlinear and regime-switching time-series models including linear VAR/VECM and threshold TVAR/TVECM with associated cointegration tests.
tools.name: tsDyn

tools.description: Identifies VAR impulse responses using a high-frequency external instrument (proxy-SVAR / Gertler-Karadi), extending models fit with the vars package.
tools.name: varexternalinstrument

tools.description: Estimation, lag selection, diagnostics, forecasting, Granger causality, IRFs and FEVD for VAR models plus SVAR and SVEC estimation (Pfaff).
tools.name: vars

tools.description: Identifies structural shocks in Bayesian VARs via sign restrictions (Uhlig rejection and penalty, Rubio-Ramirez QR, Fry-Pagan median target).
tools.name: VARsignR

tools.description: Provides robust and clustered variance-covariance estimators as a backend for Julia regression packages.
tools.name: Vcov.jl

tools.description: Julia VAR/BVAR/FAVAR estimation with IRF identification (Cholesky, long-run, sign restrictions) and asymptotic/bootstrap confidence bands.
tools.name: VectorAutoregressions.jl

tools.description: Weak-instrument-robust tests and confidence sets (AR, CLR, K) for IV/probit/tobit models (Finlay, Magnusson & Schaffer).
tools.name: weakiv

tools.description: A modern, lightweight RIM (iterative raking) library for weighting survey/people data, a fork-style successor to quantipy's weighting.
tools.name: weightipy

tools.description: Generates balancing weights (propensity scores, entropy balancing, CBPS, energy balancing) for binary, multi-category and continuous treatments.
tools.name: WeightIt

tools.description: Estimates the Vevea and Hedges (1995) weight-function model to assess and correct for publication bias in meta-analysis.
tools.name: weightr

tools.description: Computes weighted descriptive statistics and tests (weighted correlations, t-tests, chi-squared) plus weighted graphics for survey data.
tools.name: weights

tools.description: Fast wild cluster bootstrap algorithms for inference on OLS coefficients in Python.
tools.name: wildboottest

tools.description: Julia engine for fast wild (cluster) bootstrap tests and confidence sets, used as the backend for boottest and fwildclusterboot.
tools.name: WildBootTests.jl

tools.description: Stata (SSC) command estimating fixed/random-effects spatial panel models (SAR, SEM, Durbin, dynamic) by quasi-maximum likelihood with direct/indirect/total effects (Belotti, Hughes & Piano Mortari).
tools.name: xsmle

tools.description: A multi-agent framework with five cooperating agents that autonomously complete Kaggle tabular competitions across six pipeline phases.

tools.description: NeurIPS 2024 method that automatically writes comprehensive literature surveys via retrieval, parallel subsection drafting by specialized LLMs, and iterative refinement with automated evaluation.

tools.description: An open data-synthesis + agent-training recipe yielding generalist data-analytic LLMs (DataMind-7B/14B) that do multi-step, code-based reasoning over CSV/Excel/SQLite.

tools.description: An agentic LLM (DeepAnalyze-8B) that autonomously runs the end-to-end data-science pipeline from raw structured/semi-structured/unstructured data to analyst-grade research reports.
